method_named: α-fair (p-mean) scalarisation sweep over a curated ladder of stance points, with CMA-ES as the inner simulation-based policy-search solver against the commissioned simulator. AUGMECON2-style ε-constraint framing is kept as a drop-in equivalent inner formulation for stances that the board prefers to express as floors.
method_gold: alpha-approximate portfolio of policies — a small finite set of policies provably alpha-close to optimal simultaneously across all p-mean welfare functions for all p <= 1, constructed via a line-search over p values (p-MEANPORTFOLIO algorithm) with an oracle-budget-constrained heuristic variant
gold_family: Multi-objective Reinforcement Learning (MORL) over generalised p-mean social welfare functions (Egalitarian p=-inf, Nash p=0, Utilitarian p=1)
match_rationale: The recommendation identifies the exact gold family — generalised p-mean / α-fair social welfare scalarisation — and explicitly maps the board's three stance points onto the canonical Egalitarian (α→∞ / p=-∞), Nash (α=1 / p=0), and Utilitarian (α=0 / p=1) limits of that family. The structural deliverable is correct: a small curated portfolio of stance-indexed policies, exactly as the gold method produces. However, the recommendation does not name the specific gold sub-technique (the p-MEANPORTFOLIO line-search algorithm of Kim et al. 2025) and does not invoke its theoretical contribution — simultaneous α-approximation across all p ≤ 1. Instead it proposes an α-ladder sweep with CMA-ES per stance, which is a structurally-similar but theoretically weaker route (Pareto-efficiency per stance only, no simultaneous-approximation guarantee across the full p-spectrum). Family match is full; sub-technique is adjacent rather than exact.
